mnemonics: POLYMORPHISM: Parents Offer Love; Youth Makes Requests. (The Parent reference decides which child's method is dispatched at runtime)
keyPoints: Dynamic method dispatch is the mechanism by which a call to an overridden method is resolved at runtime, not compile time.
It is the core mechanism that enables Java's runtime polymorphism.
It requires an IS-A relationship (inheritance) and method overriding.
commonMistakes: Trying to use dynamic dispatch on static or final methods, which cannot be overridden.
Attempting to call a method on a parent reference that is not defined in the parent class, even if the child class has it.
Confusing compile-time type (reference type) with runtime type (object type) when determining which method is called.
